task: Record a maintenance fee charge per household and list all charges for a household via an append-only ledger backed by SQL Server.

key_reasoning.reason: Full-stack greenfield feature: new Domain record type(s), Application port interface (IMaintenanceFeeStore), two use-case classes (RecordCharge, ListCharges), SQL adapter (SqlMaintenanceFeeStore), Minimal API endpoints, Program.cs wiring, and schema extension. ISession and HouseholdRef currently live in Harmonia.Application.Reservations and Harmonia.Domain.Reservations — using them from a second feature touches shared utilities with no established cross-feature precedent, triggering the red-flag bump.

Task description is under 50 words and leaves critical questions open: amount/currency data type (decimal vs. money), presence of description or period fields, who may record a charge (any resident or an admin role only — ISession only exposes IsResident), error/retry contract for RecordCharge, and ChargeId structure. Vague acceptance criteria bump applied.

Technical analysis estimates 10–14 files: new domain file(s), Application ports file, RecordCharge and ListCharges use-case classes, SqlMaintenanceFeeStore adapter, MaintenanceFeeEndpoints static class, Program.cs (modified), db/schema.sql (modified), unit test fakes, use-case unit test class(es), endpoint translation tests, log-exclusion test, and integration test class for the SQL adapter. Maps to XL (11–15 files).

Four distinct architectural layers touched: Domain (new charge record/value types), Service/Application (port interface and two use cases), Repository/Adapter (SQL Server store adapter), API (Minimal API endpoints and route registration), plus DB-Persistence (schema.sql DDL addition).

red_flags_applied: Requirements Clarity bumped from L(4) to XL(5): task description is under 50 words with vague acceptance criteria — amount type, recorder role (resident vs. admin), error contract, and charge fields are all unspecified.

Component Scope bumped from L(4) to XL(5): ISession and HouseholdRef are currently namespace-scoped to Harmonia.Application.Reservations and Harmonia.Domain.Reservations — the new feature must consume them as shared utilities, and no multi-feature precedent exists in the codebase for how to share them.
